\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The Haddon Matrix is a framework used to evaluate injury prevention by analyzing hazards before, during, and after an incident, focusing on modifying individual, environmental, and policy-level factors—consistent with the documented risk factors for child maltreatment (e.g., parental substance use, poverty, violence) and preventive interventions like multidisciplinary team approaches discussed in the evidence. While several topics relate to maternal/infant health, only injury prevention directly aligns with the core application of the Haddon Matrix.
